chief_complaint: Two patients, aged 72 and 78, presented with life-threatening gastrointestinal bleeding while receiving amivantamab plus lazertinib for EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer.
history: Both patients had progressive hypoalbuminemia and gastrointestinal mucosal edema preceding the onset of bleeding. They received apixaban and had a history of older age and treatment in later lines of therapy.
exam: Vital signs were not provided, but both patients were evaluated with contrast-enhanced computed tomography and endoscopic evaluation.
labs: Contrast-enhanced computed tomography and endoscopic evaluation demonstrated diffuse bowel wall edema and multiple mucosal ulcers.
correct_diagnosis: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to bowel wall edema and mucosal ulcers secondary to amivantamab plus lazertinib therapy
differentials: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to bowel wall edema and mucosal ulcers secondary to amivantamab plus lazertinib therapy
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to anticoagulant therapy
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to bowel obstruction
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to ischemic colitis
correct_ranking: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding due to bowel wall edema and mucosal ulcers secondary to amivantamab plus lazertinib therapy
